🎮 private (carry) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 0-4 into a numbers advantage.
We took Primal Fangtooth in the same window — read it as a trade, not a clean loss.
Enemy had 1 healer; only 1 of us carried anti-heal — usually wants two vs a heal comp.
Their main damage was Boris (47k physical); 2/5 of us itemized armor.
Enemy ran 5/5 on or near their meta core — they drafted and built optimally; match it.

draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (support→offlane).

coaching.headline: Won on towers (10–4) and Cuban's 17/4 Sparrow despite losing the objective count — we converted leads to structures, which is what wins.
coaching.team: Classic good conversion: objectives were 14–17 against us, but we out-towered them 10–4 and Cuban snowballed his lane. Anti-heal was light (1 vs Narbash) and fine. The shape is right — take what fights we win straight to towers.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Keep converting kills to towers — that's why we won despite the neutral deficit. A second anti-heal would've made the Narbash fights faster.

perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Sparrow 17/4/1 into a favored Revenant — the win condition the tower conversion (10-4) keyed off.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Aurora jungle, 13 assists — elite enabling that set up Cuban's 17/4 Sparrow.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Grux offlane into an unfavored Bayle (5/4/3) — held the lane while the team converted to a 10-4 tower win.
perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Riktor support (15 assists) — heavy enabling that set up Cuban's 17/4 Sparrow to snowball into towers.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Argus mid into an unfavored Renna (3/5/6) — a steady fill line in a win driven by tower conversion (10-4).

threats: Chain-CC risk (1 hard-CC abilities, ~0.5s of lockdown): Bayle Kingslayer (knockback). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Narbash. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — we brought none.
synergy: Our engage/CC: Riktor Riplash (pull), Riktor Shock Therapy (silence 0.8s), Riktor Skewer (suppress 1.25s), Grux Crush (knockup 0.5s). Our kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
We have sustain (Riktor) — favor the longer fight where our heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Riplash pull → Shock Therapy silence → Skewer suppress ult
ourKits.winCondition: pull a target in and chain silence+suppress for a guaranteed kill
ourKits.playWith: burst that deletes his pulled/suppressed target
ourKits.playAgainst: respect Riplash range — stay spaced; cleanse/QSS the suppress

ourKits.combo: Smash 'n Grab pull → Crush knockup → Warlord's Challenge stun → bleed-stacked basics
ourKits.winCondition: land the pull→knockup chain to lock a target while bleed and AS shred them
ourKits.passive: basics stack a bleed for sustained damage
ourKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his knockup/stun window
ourKits.playAgainst: telegraphed engage — bait Crush/Pull, then punish; kite his slow start

ourKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
ourKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
ourKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
ourKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

ourKits.combo: Boreal Sweep slow → Hoarfrost root → Glacial Charge engage → Cryoseism stun ult
ourKits.winCondition: chain frost CC (slow→root→stun) to lock the fight down
ourKits.playWith: burst that converts her long CC chain into kills
ourKits.playAgainst: she's a CC battery — cleanse/QSS the Cryoseism stun; spread to avoid the chain

ourKits.combo: Dread Nova stun → Ether Crystal push/lift → Particle Shredder channel → Synaptic Obliterator beams
ourKits.winCondition: poke from max range and zone with the crystal
ourKits.playWith: a frontline to protect his stationary channel
ourKits.playAgainst: dodge Dread Nova; dive him during the immobile ult channel

enemyKits.combo: Thunk stun → March! speed → Song heal aura → Crash Bang Boom! AoE slow ult
enemyKits.winCondition: engage with Thunk and group-buff the team through fights
enemyKits.playWith: a dive team that rides his speed and heal
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge/cleanse Thunk; he's low-damage — kill his carry through the aura

enemyKits.combo: Detection Protocol track → Chrono Swipe → Primeval Roar shield → Ursine Burst grab ult
enemyKits.winCondition: grab a key target out of position and burst them down
enemyKits.playWith: a team that collapses on his grabbed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: his grab ult is the whole threat — bait it / stay spaced; CC during Primeval Roar

enemyKits.combo: Skull Crusher gap-close → Dire Howl slow → Cursed Edge → Kingslayer knockback dash, Berserk at low HP
enemyKits.winCondition: engage, drop low into Berserk and out-brawl
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up while he commits the dive
enemyKits.playAgainst: his Berserk rewards low HP — burst him fully or disengage; anti-heal helps

enemyKits.combo: Scar mark → Obliterate homing missiles → Reckoning 1v1 realm ult
enemyKits.winCondition: Reckoning a target into the Nether Realm and crit them down 1v1
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up if his 1v1 stalls
enemyKits.playAgainst: the ult is a 1v1 — peel the realmed ally can't help, so don't be caught alone; he's crit-reliant early-weak

enemyKits.combo: Soulfire waves → Mortal Coil pull-zone → Stupor orb → Abyssal Rift zone
enemyKits.winCondition: zone a fight with Mortal Coil + ult and chunk from range
enemyKits.passive: consumes essences from kills for power
enemyKits.playWith: frontline to hold enemies in her zones
enemyKits.playAgainst: fragile and reliant on her zones — flank/dive around the telegraphs

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
